---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Lester"
date: "2022-12-04"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(rjags)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
#library(pictograph)
source("C:/Users/Leste/Documents/GitHub/pictograph/R/bic.R")
source("C:/Users/Leste/Documents/GitHub/pictograph/R/cluster_separately.R")
source("C:/Users/Leste/Documents/GitHub/pictograph/R/clustering_functions.R")
get.parameter.chain <- function(param, chains) {
  chains[grep(paste0(param, "\\["), chains$Parameter), ]
}

```

## R Markdown
```{r cars}
clusterSep <- function(input_data,
                       n.iter = 10000, n.burn = 1000, thin = 10, mc.cores = 1,
                       inits = list(".RNG.name" = "base::Wichmann-Hill",
                                    ".RNG.seed" = 123),
                       max_K = 10,
                       model_type = "spike_and_slab",
                       beta.prior = FALSE) {
  # 1. separate mutations by sample presence
  #sep_list <- separateMutationsBySamplePresence(input_data)
  
  
  # 2a. For each presence set, run clustering MCMC, calc BIC and choose best K (min BIC)
  #all_set_results <- vector("list", length(sep_list))
  #names(all_set_results) <- names(sep_list)
  params = c("z", "w", "ystar")
  
  ####### very important to run mcmc #######
  input_data$mutation_indices <- 1:input_data$I
  
  #for (i in seq_len(length(sep_list))) {
    #temp_box <- sep_list[[4]]
    temp_box <- input_data
    # Max number of clusters cannot be more than number of mutations
    temp_max_K <- min(max_K, temp_box$I)
    
    temp_samps_list <- runMutSetMCMC(temp_box, 
                                     n.iter = n.iter, n.burn = n.burn, thin = thin, 
                                     mc.cores = mc.cores,
                                     inits = inits,
                                     temp_max_K = temp_max_K,
                                     model_type = model_type,
                                     params = params,
                                     beta.prior = beta.prior)
    #all_set_results[[i]] <- temp_samps_list
  #}
  
  return(temp_samps_list)
}
```

# Check AIC to select best K

## Including Plots
```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
input_data <- readRDS("C:/Users/Leste/Desktop/Karchin Lab/data/pictograph-input/IP22_input_data.rds")

tt <- as.data.frame(input_data$y)%>%
  cbind(as.data.frame(input_data$n))

all_set_results <- clusterSep(input_data,
                              n.iter = 1000, n.burn = 100, thin = 1,
                              max_K = 10)
chains <- all_set_results$best_chains
plotChainsCCF(chains$w_chain)

sets <- writeClusterAssignmentsTable(chains$z_chain)%>%
  mutate(Mut_ID = as.numeric(gsub("Mut","",Mut_ID)))%>%
  arrange(Mut_ID)
mut <- input_data$Mut_ID%>%
  cbind(sets)%>%
  rename("Mut" = ".")
  filter(grepl('KRAS|GNAS',Mut, ignore.case = TRUE))
```



```{r}
w_mat <- estimateCCFs(chains$w_chain)
graph_G_pre <- prepareGraphForGabowMyers(w_mat, chains, input_data)
graph_G <- filterEdgesBasedOnCCFs(graph_G_pre, w_mat, thresh = 0.98)
enumerateSpanningTreesModified(graph_G, w_mat, sum_filter_thresh=1)
scores <- calcTreeScores(chains$w_chain, all_spanning_trees)
best_tree <- all_spanning_trees[[which.max(scores)]]
plotTree(best_tree)
plotEnsembleTree(all_spanning_trees)
subclone_props <- calcSubcloneProportions(w_mat, best_tree)
subclone_props[subclone_props<0]=0
subclone_props_n <- sapply(c(1,2,3), function(x) round(subclone_props[,x]/sum(subclone_props[,x]),digits = 5))
plotSubclonePie(subclone_props_n)
plotSubcloneBar(subclone_props_n)
```
